reasoning: Both models declared the package safe but failed to audit the source code tarball containing Rust implementation. The critical executable code (build.rs, main.rs) and cryptographic operations were never reviewed.
coverage_issues: Source tarball (moshpit-keygen-0.9.2.tar.gz) skipped by both models despite containing build.rs and Rust source files
No review of cryptographic implementation in keygen functionality
Critical build-time files like build.rs and Cargo.toml not examined
false_positives: Shell completion scripts (fish/bash) flagged for review despite being low-risk static configs
Service files reviewed despite being inert systemd configs
false_negatives: Unverified cryptographic operations in Rust source code
Potential unsafe Rust code patterns in network/filesystem access
build.rs execution risks during cargo build
re_audit_focus: Extracted source files: build.rs, Cargo.toml, agent/src/main.rs
learnings: Prioritize build artifacts: Always extract/review source archives containing build scripts
Improve Rust awareness: Auto-prioritize build.rs, Cargo.toml, and main entrypoints
Filter false positives: De-prioritize static configs (completion scripts, service files)
Handle binary files: Implement source extraction for compressed archives
